title: L2: Lights and Drums
description: Representing characters as sequences of symbols and carrying those symbols on physical media.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from Lesson 1. Each question gives the choices you need.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-dna',questions:[{"prompt":"What is a representation?","answers":["a way of showing information","way of showing information","showing information"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A message tells someone the room number. Type A, B or C.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sign. Which is best for rows of names and scores?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A saved note is checked tomorrow. Type A, B or C.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Characters as Symbols
steps.content:
Characters as sequences of symbols

A character is one letter, digit, space or punctuation mark. A coding scheme gives each character a symbol sequence. The sequence must be agreed so the sender and receiver understand the same message.

Coding schemeSymbols usedExample to read
Morse codeDots and dashesS = ... and O = ---
BrailleRaised dot patternsDifferent raised dots mean different characters
ASCIIBinary number codesA character is stored as a number
UnicodeBinary number codesMore characters and languages can be represented

To measure length, count the symbols in the representation you are looking at. For example, 101101 has six binary symbols.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-intro-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = character, B = coding scheme, C = physical medium. Morse code is which one?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: Morse code is a coding scheme."},{"prompt":"A = character, B = coding scheme, C = physical medium. A light flash is which one?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: light can carry symbols physically."}]});

steps.title: Key Ideas: Characters, Codes and Media
steps.content:
Key ideas

A character is a single letter, digit, punctuation mark or space. A coding scheme tells us which sequence of symbols represents each character. A physical medium carries those symbols in the real world.

IdeaMeaningExample
CharacterOne item of textA, 7, ? or a space
Symbol sequenceSymbols placed in order... or 1010
Coding schemeRules that match characters to sequencesMorse code, Braille, ASCII, Unicode
Physical mediumHow the symbols are carriedLight, sound, ink, raised dots, voltage

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-vocab-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = character, B = symbol sequence, C = physical medium. The letter A is which one?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: A is a character."},{"prompt":"A = character, B = symbol sequence, C = physical medium. The pattern .- is which one?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: .- is a sequence of symbols."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: SOS
steps.content:
Step through Morse code

Click through slowly: start with characters, change them into symbols, then count the symbols.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr2-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"Start with characters","html":"

S O S

","note":"There are three characters: S, O and S."},{"title":"Use the coding scheme","html":"
CharacterMorse sequence
S...
O---
S...
","note":"Morse code changes each character into dots and dashes."},{"title":"Join the sequences","html":"

... --- ...

","note":"The full message is now represented using dot and dash symbols."},{"title":"Count the symbols","html":"

3 dots + 3 dashes + 3 dots = 9 symbols

","note":"Spaces help humans read the message, but here we are counting only dots and dashes."},{"title":"Choose a physical medium","html":"
MediumHow it carries the symbols
LightShort and long flashes
SoundShort and long beeps
DrumShort and long beats
","note":"The same dot and dash sequence can be carried in different physical ways."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"In Morse code, S is ... and O is ---. How many dot/dash symbols are in SOS?","answer":"9","right":"Correct: 3 + 3 + 3 = 9."},{"prompt":"A = light, B = coding rule, C = character. Which can carry Morse code physically?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: light can carry the symbols."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: From Character to Medium
steps.content:
Step through the full journey

Follow the journey from a letter to a physical signal.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr2-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"Start with a character","html":"

A

","note":"The character is what we want to represent."},{"title":"Choose a coding scheme","html":"
Coding schemeExample idea
Morse codeA is dot dash
ASCIIA has a number code
","note":"The coding scheme gives the rule."},{"title":"Write the symbol sequence","html":"

.-

","note":"In Morse code, A can be represented by dot dash."},{"title":"Carry it physically","html":"
MediumWhat happens
LightShort flash then long flash
SoundShort beep then long beep
","note":"The same symbols can travel using light or sound."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = character, B = code sequence, C = medium. In the example, .- is which one?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: it is the symbol sequence."},{"prompt":"A = light, B = ASCII, C = Braille. Which one is a physical medium?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: light is a medium."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: Count Before You Answer
steps.content:
Mini check: counting symbols

Count exactly what the question asks for. If it asks for characters, count letters. If it asks for binary symbols, count 0s and 1s. If it asks for dot/dash symbols, count dots and dashes.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-mini-check',questions:[{"prompt":"How many characters are in CAT?","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in .- ?","answer":"2","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many binary symbols are in 0101?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many characters are in A7?","answer":"2","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in --... ?","answer":"5","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: count symbols

Count the symbols shown in the question. Type only the number.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-practice-a',questions:[{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in ... ?","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in --- ?","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in ...---... ?","answer":"9","right":"Correct: 3 + 3 + 3 = 9."},{"prompt":"How many characters are in HELLO?","answer":"5","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many binary symbols are in 101101?","answer":"6","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: schemes and media

Use the labelled options. Type A, B or C.

LetterAnswer
AMorse code
BBraille
CASCII or Unicode
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-practice-b',questions:[{"prompt":"Which option uses dots and dashes?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: Morse code uses dots and dashes."},{"prompt":"Which option uses raised dot patterns?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: Braille uses raised dots."},{"prompt":"Which option uses binary number codes for characters?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: ASCII and Unicode use binary number codes."},{"prompt":"A = light flashes, B = written rules, C = number of symbols. Which one is a physical medium?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: light flashes can carry symbols."},{"prompt":"A = coding scheme, B = physical medium, C = character. What is Morse code?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: Morse code is a coding scheme."}]});

steps.title: Extension: Match Code to Medium
steps.content:
Extension challenge

These questions mix the lesson ideas. Use A, B or C where choices are given.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-extension',questions:[{"prompt":"A = coding scheme, B = physical medium, C = character. What is Unicode?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: Unicode is a coding scheme."},{"prompt":"A = coding scheme, B = physical medium, C = character. What is a light flash?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: light can physically carry symbols."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which would be read by touch?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: Braille uses raised dots."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which represents characters using binary number codes?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated questions correctly to complete this step.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"How many symbols are in 11001010?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many characters are in CODE?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which uses dots and dashes?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which uses raised dot patterns?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = light flashes, B = coding rules, C = character length. Which is a physical medium?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer all questions.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr2-exam',questions:[{"prompt":"A coding scheme is a set of agreed rules for matching characters to what?","answers":["symbols","symbol sequences","sequences of symbols"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = Braille, C = ASCII/Unicode. Which uses binary number codes?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many symbols are in --.. ?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = light flashes, B = coding scheme, C = character. Which can physically carry symbols?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many binary symbols are in 1001?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."}]});

quizQuestions.q: What does a character coding scheme do?
options: It matches characters to symbol sequences
